heading: Grade on retirement
label: 2501 Grade on retirement

Modifications and Revisions Relating to Reopening Retired Grade Determinations–(d), , , provided that:Pub. L. 119–60, div. G, title LXXII, § 7224(b)139 Stat. 1703Dec. 18, 2025“(b).—Issuance and Revision of Regulations Relating to Good Cause to Reopen Retired Grade DeterminationsNot later than 180 days after the date of enactment of this Act [], the Secretary of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating shall issue or revise, as applicable, and at the discretion of the Secretary consistent with this section, regulations of the Coast Guard to do the following:Dec. 18, 2025“(1)Define what constitutes good cause to reopen a retired grade determination referred to in subparagraph (H) of , United States Code, as redesignated by subsection (a), to ensure that the following shall be considered good cause for such a reopening:section 2501(d)(2) of title 14“(A)Circumstances that constitute a failure to carry out applicable laws regarding a report of sexual assault with an intent to deceive by a commissioned officer, that relate to a response made to a report of sexual assault, during the commissioned service of the officer.“(B)Substantial evidence of sexual assault by the commissioned officer concerned, at any time during the commissioned service of such officer, or such evidence that was not considered by the Coast Guard in a manner consistent with law.“(2)Identify the standard for making, and the evidentiary showing required to support, an adverse determination on the retired grade of a commissioned officer.“(c).—Revision of Limitations on Reopening Retired Grade DeterminationsNot later than 180 days after the date of enactment of this Act, the Secretary of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating shall revise applicable guidance in section K.10 of chapter 3 of Commandant Instruction 1000.4A to remove any restriction that limits the ability to reopen the retired grade of a commissioned officer based on—“(1)whether new evidence is discovered contemporaneously with or within a short time period after the date of retirement of the officer concerned; and“(2)whether the misconduct concerned was not discoverable through due diligence.“(d).—Savings ClauseNo provision of this section [amending this section and enacting this note] or the amendments made by this section shall be construed to permit a review of conduct that was not in violation of law or policy at the time of the alleged conduct.”

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: A commissioned officer who is retired under any provision of this title, shall be retired from active service with the highest grade held by the commissioned officer for not less than six months while on active duty in which, as determined by the Secretary, the commissioned officer’s performance of duty was satisfactory.

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary may conditionally determine the highest grade of satisfactory service of the commissioned officer pending completion of the investigation; and

children.label: B
children.text: the grade under subparagraph (A) is subject to resolution under subsection (c)(2).

children.heading: .—Warrant Officers
children.label: b .—Warrant Officers
children.text: Any warrant officer who is retired under any provision of section 580, 1263, 1293, or 1305 of title 10, shall be retired from active service with the highest commissioned grade above chief warrant officer, W–4, held by the warrant officer for not less than six months on active duty in which, as determined by the Secretary, the warrant officer’s performance of duty was satisfactory.

children.heading: .—Misconduct in lower grade
children.label: 1 .—Misconduct in lower grade
children.text: In the case of a commissioned officer whom the Secretary determines committed misconduct in a lower grade, the Secretary may determine the commissioned officer has not served satisfactorily in any grade equal to or higher than that lower grade.

children.heading: .—Adverse findings
children.label: 2 .—Adverse findings
children.text: A determination of the retired grade of a commissioned officer shall be resolved following a conditional determination under subsection (a)(2) if the investigation of or personnel action against the commissioned officer results in adverse findings.

children.heading: .—Recalculation of retired pay
children.label: 3 .—Recalculation of retired pay
children.text: If the retired grade of a commissioned officer is reduced pursuant to this subsection, the retired pay of the commissioned officer shall be recalculated under chapter 71 of title 10, and any modification of the retired pay of the commissioned officer shall go into effect on the effective date of the reduction in retired grade.

children.heading: Finality of Retired Grade Determinations.—
children.label: d Finality of Retired Grade Determinations.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), a determination of the retired grade of a commissioned officer under this section is administratively final on the day the commissioned officer is retired, and may not be reopened.

children.label: A
children.text: the retirement or retired grade of the commissioned officer was procured by fraud;

children.label: B
children.text: substantial evidence comes to light after the retirement that could have led to a lower retired grade under this section and such evidence was not known by a competent authority at the time of retirement;

children.label: C
children.text: substantial evidence comes to light that, during the commissioned service of the officer, the officer failed to carry out applicable laws, with an intent to deceive or defraud;

children.label: D
children.text: substantial evidence comes to light after the retirement that the officer committed rape or sexual assault, as described in sections 920(a) and 920(b) of title 10 (articles 120(a) and 120(b) of the Uniform Code of Military Justice) at any time during the commissioned service of the officer;

children.label: E
children.text: substantial evidence comes to light after the retirement that the commissioned officer knew of and failed to report through proper channels, in accordance with existing law at the time of the alleged incident, any known instances of sexual assault by a member of the Coast Guard under the command of the officer during the officer’s service;

children.label: F
children.text: a mistake of law or calculation was made in the determination of the retired grade;

children.label: G
children.text: in the case of a retired grade following a conditional determination under subsection (a)(2), the investigation of or personnel action against the commissioned officer results in adverse findings; or

children.label: H
children.text: the Secretary determines, under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, that good cause exists to reopen the determination.

children.label: A
children.text: shall notify the commissioned officer of the reopening; and

children.label: B
children.text: may not make an adverse determination on the retired grade of the commissioned officer until the commissioned officer has had a reasonable opportunity to respond regarding the basis of the reopening.

children.heading: .—Recalculation of retired pay
children.label: 4 .—Recalculation of retired pay
children.text: If the retired grade of a commissioned officer is reduced through the reopening of the commissioned officer’s retired grade under paragraph (2), the retired pay of the commissioned officer shall be recalculated under chapter 71 of title 10, and any modification of the retired pay of the commissioned officer shall go into effect on the effective date of the reduction in retired grade.

children.heading: .—Inapplicability to Commissioned Warrant Officers
children.label: e .—Inapplicability to Commissioned Warrant Officers
children.text: This section, including subsection (b), shall not apply to commissioned warrant officers.
